This may be popular because of the "That's no good" meme, but the lesson that Sonic is trying to tell the audience is SO important, and surprisingly still relevant, even to this day.

This is why we need to help children to develop healthy boundaries for body safety in order to protect themselves from sexual assault, as there are many pedophiles, rapists, child molesters, sadists, predators, and similar criminals out there who are looking to kidnap, torture, rape, or even murder these young ones.

...And such kidnapping, torturing, sexually assaulting is increasing the victims' risk of suffering from mental health issues later in life, such as PTSD, depression, anxiety, OCD, and other mental health conditions.

Parents have a part to play in protecting their children from sexual assault. By helping them to develop healthy boundaries for their bodies, teaching bodies parts and teaching consent, this can help increase their resilience and reduce their risk of mental health issues.

text: She's in the Christmas special that was delayed until 1996 to coincide with the release of the Sonic 3D Blast game. That special was mostly AoStH mixed with traces of SatAM, including Sally in her original pink form and early design Swatbots. This episode was supposed to originally air prior to SatAM's debut mere days following AoStH starting, but it was delayed on purpose because the original title--Sonic's Xtreme Christmas==was to coincide with the release of the scrapped Sonic Xtreme game.
author: @TherealRNOwwfpooh

text: This is Classic Sonic, remember? Back when "Steve Urkel" (Jaleel White) did the voice, just as wrestling manager Cap'n Lou Albano portrayed a live-action Mario on The Super Mario Bros. Super Show. The pro-Japanese post-Sonic Adventure canons didn't yet exist. Only the Genesis era games and the Western continuties from DiC (cartoons) and Archie (comics) existed, as did the UK Sonic comic by Fleetway that was overtaken in sales by Archie's version.
author: @TherealRNOwwfpooh

text: Yes. But in AoStH, he was his game age (8). Sonic, as Looney Tunes-esque as AoStH tried to be, remainee 15-16 (his game age), as well. Besides, Sally appeared in her classic pink form in the delayed-until-1996 Christmas episode (released the same year as Sonic 3D Blast, which used the engine of the scrapped Sonic 16/Sonic Mars [SatAM prototype] & Sonic Xtreme titles), which was basically a mesh of AoStH with traces of SatAM's early concepts.
author: @TherealRNOwwfpooh
